samples.description: Major radius (R) coordinate of geometric axis.
samples.rationale: Major radius coordinate of the geometric axis is a well-defined physical/geometric quantity describing the plasma equilibrium.

samples.description: Vertical (Z) coordinate of geometric axis.
samples.rationale: Vertical coordinate of the geometric axis is a well-defined physical/geometric quantity of the plasma equilibrium.

samples.description: 2D geometry specification for object.
samples.rationale: This is a structural/representational container for GGD grid geometry specification, not a measurable physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for time slice.
samples.rationale: This is a bare coordinate array (time base), not a physical quantity warranting a standard name.

samples.description: First dimension of grid.
samples.rationale: This is a bare coordinate/grid dimension array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Major radius (R) coordinate of outline.
samples.rationale: The major radius coordinates of the separatrix boundary outline describe a physically meaningful geometric property of the plasma boundary.

samples.description: Covariant metric tensor components defined on each element of a GGD grid subset base for the equilibrium representation. Used to map between curvilinear equilibrium coordinates and Cartesian frame in GGD grid calculations.
samples.rationale: The covariant metric tensor has physical meaning (mapping between coordinate systems), but it is primarily a representational/computational artifact of the GGD grid framework rather than a directly measured or standard plasma quantity.

samples.description: Space reference for object.
samples.rationale: This is a structural reference/index into the GGD space hierarchy, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Interpolation coefficients for r.
samples.rationale: Interpolation coefficients are a representational/numerical artifact, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Chi-squared goodness of fit for mse polarisation angle.
samples.rationale: Chi-squared is a fit quality metric, not a physical quantity warranting a standard name.

samples.description: Chi-squared goodness of fit for faraday angle.
samples.rationale: Chi-squared is a fit quality metric, not a physical quantity warranting a standard name.

samples.description: Chi-squared goodness of fit for magnetization z.
samples.rationale: Chi-squared is a fit quality metric, not a physical quantity warranting a standard name.

samples.description: Minimum charge number Z (Z_min) of a bundled ion charge-state group in the 2D core plasma profiles. Defines the lower bound of the charge state range for bundled ionisation states of a given ion species.
samples.rationale: Minimum charge state number of a bundled ion species is a physically meaningful quantity characterizing the ion charge state distribution.

samples.description: Toroidal component of velocity.
samples.rationale: Toroidal electron velocity is a well-defined, measurable physical quantity.

samples.description: Ion temperature (Ti) for a specific charge state on the 2D poloidal cross-section grid of the core plasma. Represents the thermal energy of the ion state as a function of position in the poloidal plane.
samples.rationale: Ion state temperature on a 2D grid is a fundamental plasma physical quantity.

samples.description: Second dimension of grid.
samples.rationale: This is a bare coordinate/grid dimension array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for profiles 1d.
samples.rationale: This is a bare time coordinate array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for statistics.
samples.rationale: This is a bare time coordinate array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Normalised toroidal flux coordinate (ρ_tor_norm) of each measurement point used in fitting the electron temperature (T_e) radial profile. For line-integrated measurements, this is the minimum ρ_tor_norm along the line of sight.
samples.rationale: This is a coordinate array associated with a fit artifact (measurement locations for fitting), not a physical quantity itself.

samples.description: Normalised toroidal flux coordinate (ρ_tor_norm) at each measurement location used in the ion density (ni) profile fit. For line-of-sight measurements, this is the minimum ρ_tor_norm along the chord.
samples.rationale: This is a coordinate array associated with a fit artifact (measurement locations for fitting), not a physical quantity itself.

samples.description: Measured value of t i average fit.
samples.rationale: This is a fit artifact (measured value used in fitting), not a primary physical quantity deserving a standard name.

samples.description: Mean square ionic charge (⟨Z²⟩) of an ion species as a 1D radial profile in the edge/SOL region, computed as the square-charge-weighted sum over charge states divided by ion density. Used in collision frequency and transport calculations.
samples.rationale: Mean square ionic charge <Z²> is a well-defined physical quantity used in collision frequency and transport calculations.

samples.description: Diamagnetic component of velocity exb.
samples.rationale: Diamagnetic component of ExB velocity is a physically meaningful vector component of a plasma drift velocity.

samples.description: Toroidal component of j perpendicular viscosity.
samples.rationale: Toroidal component of the perpendicular viscosity current density is a well-defined physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for profiles 1d.
samples.rationale: This is a time coordinate array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Normalised poloidal flux coordinate (ρ_pol_norm) defined as sqrt((ψ−ψ_axis)/(ψ_LCFS−ψ_axis)), serving as the radial grid coordinate for 1D edge and SOL plasma profiles. Values range from 0 at the axis to 1 at the separatrix.
samples.rationale: This is a coordinate array (normalised poloidal flux coordinate used as a grid), not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for ggd.
samples.rationale: This is a time coordinate array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Interpolation coefficients for r of b field.
samples.rationale: Interpolation coefficients are representation/numerical artifacts, not physical quantities.

samples.description: Interpolation coefficients for r of velocity over b field.
samples.rationale: Interpolation coefficients are representation artifacts, not physical quantities.

samples.description: Interpolation coefficients for radial of j total.
samples.rationale: Interpolation coefficients are representation artifacts, not physical quantities.

samples.description: Weight factor for temperature fit.
samples.rationale: Weight factor for a fit is a fit artifact/metadata, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Normalised poloidal flux coordinate (ρ_pol_norm = sqrt((ψ−ψ_axis)/(ψ_LCFS−ψ_axis))) at each measurement location used in the electron temperature (Te) profile fit in the edge and SOL region.
samples.rationale: Coordinate within a fit artifact structure; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Normalised poloidal flux coordinate (rho_pol_norm) at each measurement point used in fitting the edge ion density profile. For line-of-sight measurements, this is the minimum rho_pol_norm along the line of sight.
samples.rationale: Coordinate within a fit artifact structure; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Explicit (source) part of the decomposed ion energy source term in 1D radial core transport equations. Applied directly at each solver iteration, independent of the primary quantity (ion temperature Ti).
samples.rationale: This is a solver-decomposed part of an ion energy source. It has physical meaning (explicit source contribution) but is tied to numerical decomposition conventions, making it borderline.

samples.description: Normalized z of element.
samples.rationale: Normalized atomic number (z_n) of an element is a well-defined physical/nuclear property.

samples.description: Implicit part of the total ion particle source term in the 1D radial transport equation, multiplied by the primary quantity (density) during numerical integration. Paired with explicit_part to form the full decomposed source.
samples.rationale: Implicit part of a decomposed source term is tied to numerical solver conventions rather than being a purely physical quantity.

samples.description: Normalised toroidal flux coordinate (ρ_tor_norm) for the 1D radial grid of core source profiles. Defined as ρ_tor divided by its value at the last closed flux surface (LCFS).
samples.rationale: This is a coordinate array (normalised toroidal flux coordinate used as a grid), not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Data field in core_sources.
samples.rationale: This is a time coordinate array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for global quantities.
samples.rationale: This is a time coordinate array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Array of values for b field phi.
samples.rationale: Toroidal (phi) component of the magnetic field is a fundamental physical quantity.

samples.description: Measure (area/volume) of object.
samples.rationale: Represents a geometric measure (area/volume) of a grid object, which is a physically meaningful quantity.

samples.description: Array of values for a field r.
samples.rationale: Represents the R-component of the magnetic vector potential on a GGD grid, a physically meaningful MHD field quantity.

samples.description: Time base for ggd.
samples.rationale: This is a time coordinate array, not a physical quantity deserving a standard name.

samples.description: Time base for grid ggd.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array for the grid; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Data field in mhd.
samples.rationale: Top-level time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Covariant metric tensor components (g_ij) defined on each element of a GGD grid subset, encoding local coordinate geometry for MHD calculations. Used with the contravariant tensor and Jacobian to evaluate differential operators on the unstructured grid.
samples.rationale: Covariant metric tensor components are geometrically meaningful but serve as representation/structural metadata for coordinate transformations on the grid. Could be argued either way.

samples.description: Contravariant metric tensor components (g^ij) for the canonical coordinate frame of a GGD grid subset in the MHD IDS, describing the inverse mapping from curvilinear grid coordinates to physical space. Provided per grid element alongside the covariant tensor and Jacobian.
samples.rationale: Contravariant metric tensor components are geometrically meaningful but categorized as representation; similar ambiguity as the covariant tensor.

samples.description: Contravariant metric tensor components (g^ij) defined on each element of a GGD grid subset base in the MHD IDS. Used to transform between covariant and contravariant vector or tensor representations in the MHD grid geometry.
samples.rationale: Same reasoning as other metric tensor components — physically meaningful geometric quantity but categorized as representation.

samples.description: Time base for diamagnetic flux.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for voltage.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for field.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Times at which a fringe jump correction has been applied to interferometer data at a given wavelength channel. Fringe jumps are discrete 2π phase errors in interferometric line-integrated electron density measurements.
samples.rationale: These are metadata timestamps recording when corrections were applied — provenance/processing metadata, not a measurable physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for n e line average.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for n e volume average.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for electrons n.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Pulse (shot) number identifying the experimental discharge in the equilibrium IDS used for mapping Thomson scattering measurements to spatial coordinates. Links the diagnostic data to its equilibrium reconstruction source entry.
samples.rationale: This is a provenance/metadata identifier (pulse/shot number), not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Upper wavelength bound of a spectral detection band in a Thomson scattering polychromator. Defines the high-wavelength edge of the photon collection window for a given detector channel used in Te and ne measurements.
samples.rationale: Upper wavelength bound of a spectral detection band is a physically meaningful instrumental quantity that characterizes the measurement.

samples.description: Vertical (Z) coordinate of delta position.
samples.rationale: Vertical (Z) coordinate of a position offset is a physically meaningful spatial quantity.

samples.description: Time base for delta position.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for t e.
samples.rationale: Time coordinate array; not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for n e.
samples.rationale: Time base coordinate array, not a physical quantity deserving a standard name.

samples.description: Normalized atoms of element.
samples.rationale: Normalized number of atoms of an element — a physically meaningful quantity related to recycling composition.

samples.description: Maximum charge state number (Z_max) of the ion charge state bundle for kinetic energy flux calculations at the wall surface in the GGD description. Defines the upper bound of the ionisation state range represented by this bundle.
samples.rationale: Maximum charge state number of an ion bundle — a physically meaningful quantity characterizing the ionisation state range.

samples.description: Black-body radiated power (P_BB) emitted from the first wall, accounting for surface emissivity weighting. Represents the thermal radiation contribution to wall heat loads from plasma-facing components at their current temperature.
samples.rationale: Black-body radiated power from the first wall is a well-defined, measurable physical quantity.

samples.description: Vertical (Z) coordinate of outline.
samples.rationale: Geometric coordinate (Z) of an outline — a bare coordinate array, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for material.
samples.rationale: Time base coordinate, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Major radius (R) coordinate of j total.
samples.rationale: Major radius coordinate array for j_total — a bare coordinate, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Major radius (R) coordinate of e field.
samples.rationale: Major radius coordinate array for e_field — a bare coordinate, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Interpolation coefficients for r of j total.
samples.rationale: Interpolation coefficients — representation/metadata, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Interpolation coefficients for incident.
samples.rationale: Interpolation coefficients — representation/metadata, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Interpolation coefficients for emitted.
samples.rationale: Interpolation coefficients — representation/metadata, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Average poloidal magnetic flux (ψ) produced by a specified list of active PF coils, weighted over the plasma poloidal cross-section as ∫ψ_coils·J_tor·dS / Ip. Quantifies the coil contribution to plasma flux linkage.
samples.rationale: Average poloidal magnetic flux from PF coils weighted over the plasma cross-section is a well-defined physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for force.
samples.rationale: Time base coordinate, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for force vertical.
samples.rationale: Time base coordinate, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Time base for resistance additional.
samples.rationale: Time base coordinate, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Specifies the physical coordinates associated with each dimension of a GGD space in the runaway_electrons IDS. The array of coordinate identifiers defines the dimensionality and coordinate system of the space, controlling how runaway electron field quantities are spatially located across real or flux-coordinate dimensions.
samples.rationale: Identifier/enumeration specifying coordinate system type — not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Specifies the type of geometric content stored in GGD geometry arrays for mesh elements in the distribution_sources IDS. The identifier determines whether the geometry array contains node coordinates, edge surface areas, face volumes, connection lengths, or other topological data needed to interpret fast-particle source distributions on the mesh.
samples.rationale: Identifier/enumeration specifying geometric content type — not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Specifies the geometric reference height used to define the midplane in the edge_profiles IDS, impacting how SOL and divertor profile data are mapped relative to the equatorial plane. The choice affects profile alignment in parallel transport analyses and upstream-to-target mappings in edge simulations.
samples.rationale: Identifier specifying the midplane reference — metadata/configuration, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Specifies the energy regime of neutrals contributing to the source term in the plasma_sources IDS. Cold, thermal, fast, and NBI neutral categories represent distinct populations with different penetration depths and interaction rates, which governs how the associated source term is spatially distributed in the plasma.
samples.rationale: Identifier/enumeration classifying neutral energy regime — not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Classifies the magnetic topology and overall configuration of the GGD grid used in the TF coil IDS. The selected topology — ranging from simple limiter to single null or double null divertor configurations — determines how flux-tube connectivity and boundary conditions are interpreted for quantities stored on this grid.
samples.rationale: Identifier classifying grid topology — metadata/configuration, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Identifies whether an optical element in the spectrometer_visible diagnostic is made of a metal or a dielectric material. The material type determines whether the sibling refractive_index and extinction_coefficient (metal) or refractive_index and transmission_coefficient (dielectric) fields are used to characterize the element's optical response.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration classifying the material type of an optical element, not a measurable physical quantity.

samples.description: Classifies the energy regime of neutral particles contributing to source terms in the edge_sources IDS, distinguishing cold, thermal, fast, and NBI neutrals. The neutral type determines the appropriate reaction rates and cross-sections used to compute ionization, charge exchange, and recombination source contributions.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration classifying the energy regime of neutral particles, not a measurable or computable physical quantity.

samples.description: Identifies the physical process generating fast particles as a source in the distribution function evolution equation. The classification covers NBI injection and a wide range of nuclear fusion reactions, each producing fast particles with a characteristic birth energy and velocity-space distribution that determines the initial conditions for slowing-down calculations.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration classifying the physical process generating fast particles, not a measurable quantity.

samples.description: Classifies the energy regime of neutral particles described in the distributions IDS, distinguishing cold, thermal, fast, and NBI neutrals. This classification determines the appropriate cross-sections, mean free paths, and interaction models applied to the neutral distribution in transport and heating calculations.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration classifying the energy regime of neutral particles, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Specifies the regularization algorithm used to reconstruct the neutron emissivity profile from tomographic detector measurements. The choice among Tikhonov variants (first derivative, second derivative, or Minimum Fischer) controls the smoothness constraint imposed on the reconstructed profile, affecting the balance between spatial resolution and noise suppression in the inferred fusion power distribution.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration selecting a regularization algorithm, not a measurable physical quantity.

samples.description: Identifies which plasma species type the distribution data structure refers to in the distributions IDS, selecting from electrons, ions, neutrals, neutrons, or photons. This determines which sibling ion, neutral, or species-state sub-structure is applicable and how the distribution function contributes to transport and heating models.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration selecting the species type, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Selects the 2D coordinate system used to represent plasma profile data in the poloidal plane within the plasma_profiles IDS. The grid type determines whether density, temperature, and flow profiles are stored on a real-space (R,Z) grid or on flux-surface-based coordinates with a specified poloidal angle convention, affecting both resolution and consistency with transport solvers.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration selecting the coordinate system for 2D grids, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Selects the analytical definition used to determine the critical momentum for hot-tail runaway electron generation in the runaway_electrons IDS. The choice references the Rosenbluth-Putvinski 1997 formulation and ensures traceability of the momentum threshold used during fast thermal quench events where the hot-tail mechanism dominates runaway seeding.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier selecting an analytical definition/formulation, not a measurable physical quantity.

samples.description: Specifies the thermodynamic power cycle used for energy conversion in the balance-of-plant primary loop. The choice between Brayton (gas turbine) and Rankine (steam turbine) cycles determines the working fluid, operating temperature range, and thermal efficiency of the plant, which directly affects the net electrical output calculated from the fusion heat source.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration selecting the thermodynamic power cycle type, not a physical quantity.

samples.description: Specifies what geometric data is stored in the geometry arrays for each mesh element dimension in the radiation IDS GGD structure. The choice determines whether the arrays contain node coordinates, edge surface areas, or face grid indices with volumes and connection lengths, controlling how the sibling geometry arrays are physically interpreted for radiation calculations.
samples.rationale: This is an identifier/enumeration specifying what geometric data is stored, not a physical quantity.
Provenance/metadata field (library commit hash), not a physical quantity.
Metadata/provenance field (plugin infrastructure name), not a physical quantity.
Metadata field indicating the source of the resistive time data, not a physical quantity.
Metadata field indicating the source of the LH power data, not a physical quantity.
Metadata/provenance field (plugin node path), not a physical quantity.
Metadata field describing the electron configuration of an ion state, not a measurable physical quantity.
Provenance/metadata field (timestamp), not a physical quantity.
Metadata field (name string for a neutral state), not a physical quantity.
Metadata/provenance field (plugin repository), not a physical quantity.
Metadata/provenance field (plugin infrastructure name), not a physical quantity.
Provenance/metadata field indicating the data provider; not a physical quantity.
Metadata field indicating the source of a value; not a measurable physical quantity.
Metadata/identifier field giving the name of a moving mode; not a physical quantity.
Provenance/metadata field indicating the data provider; not a physical quantity.
Identifier/enumeration for the vibrational mode of a neutral state; this is a structural/metadata label, not a measurable physical quantity.

reviewer_schema.keep: path SHOULD become a Standard Name (currently filtered out → false negative)
reviewer_schema.drop: path correctly excluded (not an SN candidate)
